a:r.a:t: This slide makes the case for why we need the harness at all. On the left side, you see the four fundamental limitations of raw LLMs. "Single-Turn Only" means the model answers one question at a time with no ability to take actions or loop back if the answer is incomplete. "No Tool Access" means it cannot search databases, execute code, or call APIs -- it only generates text. "No Memory" means every request starts from scratch with no knowledge of previous conversations. And "No Planning" means it cannot decompose complex multi-step tasks. On the right side, each limitation maps to a specific harness capability. The multi-turn loop implements the ReAct pattern where the agent can observe, think, act, and repeat until the task is done. Tool integration provides a registry of callable functions -- in our case, 55 tools across 8 groups. Session and memory gives the agent persistence across turns plus long-term memory extraction. And task orchestration enables the coordinator agent to decompose work into parallel subtasks dispatched to specialized workers. Each of these capabilities is a distinct subsystem in our codebase that we will walk through in detail.
